content:
Let N=nⁿ+1 be perfect.  Every known perfect number is even of the form  
 2^(p–1)(2^p–1) with 2^p–1 prime.  
So we seek n>0 with  
 nⁿ+1 = 2^(p–1)(2^p–1).  
Checking small p gives the perfects 6,28,496,8128,…:

p=2:6 ⇒ nⁿ=5 no solution  
p=3:28 ⇒ nⁿ=27 ⇒ n=3  
p=5:496 ⇒ nⁿ=495 no integral n  
p=7:8128 ⇒ nⁿ=8127 no integral n  
…  

No larger p can work (nⁿ grows too fast, and no odd perfects are known), so the only solution is  

\boxed{3}.
